briefdescription.para: Unknown layer code, do not use.
Network interface layer code.
Connectivity layer code.
IPv4 layer code.
IPv6 layer code.
L4 layer code.
CoAP layer code.
Statistics layer code.
Hostap (wpa_supplicant) layer code.
Ethernet layer code.
IEEE 802.15.4 layer code.
PPP layer code.
Virtual network interface layer code.
Wi-Fi layer code.
User layer code 3.
User layer code 2.
User layer code 1.
Reserved layer code for future use.
Central place the definition of the layer codes (7 bit value)

memberdef.argsstring: )(uint64_t mgmt_request, struct net_if *iface, void *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: net_mgmt_request_handler_t

briefdescription.para: Signature which all Net MGMT request handler need to follow.

parameterdescription.para: The exact request value the handler is being called through
A valid pointer on struct net_if if the request is meant to be tied to a network interface. NULL otherwise.
A valid pointer on a data understood by the handler. NULL otherwise.
Length in byte of the memory pointed by data.

briefdescription.para: Define the user's callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: Original struct net_mgmt_event_callback owning this handler.
The network event being notified.
A pointer on a struct net_if to which the event belongs to, if it's an event on an iface. NULL otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: )(uint64_t mgmt_event, struct net_if *iface, void *info, size_t info_length, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: net_mgmt_event_static_handler_t

briefdescription.para: Define the user's callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: The network event being notified.
A pointer on a struct net_if to which the event belongs to, if it's an event on an iface. NULL otherwise.
A valid pointer on a data understood by the handler. NULL otherwise.
Length in bytes of the memory pointed by info.
Data provided by the user to the handler.

briefdescription.para: Helper to initialize a struct net_mgmt_event_callback properly.

parameterdescription.para: A valid application's callback structure pointer.
A valid handler function pointer.
A mask of relevant events for the handler

briefdescription.para: Add a user callback.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on user's callback to add.

briefdescription.para: Delete a user callback.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on user's callback to delete.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t mgmt_event, struct net_if *iface, const void *info, size_t length)
memberdef.name: net_mgmt_event_notify_with_info

briefdescription.para: Used by the system to notify an event.

detaileddescription.para:
mgmt_event
The actual network event code to notify
iface
a valid pointer on a struct net_if if only the event is based on an iface. NULL otherwise.
info
A valid pointer on the information you want to pass along with the event. NULL otherwise. Note the data pointed there is normalized by the related event.
length
size of the data pointed by info pointer.
Note: info and length are disabled if CONFIG_NET_MGMT_EVENT_INFO is not defined.

briefdescription.para: Used by the system to notify an event without any additional information.

parameterdescription.para: The actual network event code to notify
A valid pointer on a struct net_if if only the event is based on an iface. NULL otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t mgmt_event_mask, uint64_t *raised_event, struct net_if **iface, const void **info, size_t *info_length, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: net_mgmt_event_wait

briefdescription.para: Used to wait synchronously on an event mask.

parameterdescription.para: A mask of relevant events to wait on.
a pointer on a uint32_t to get which event from the mask generated the event. Can be NULL if the caller is not interested in that information.

a pointer on a place holder for the iface on which the event has originated from. This is valid if only the event mask has bit NET_MGMT_IFACE_BIT set relevantly, depending on events the caller wants to listen to.
a valid pointer if user wants to get the information the event might bring along. NULL otherwise.
tells how long the info memory area is. Only valid if the info is not NULL.
A timeout delay. K_FOREVER can be used to wait indefinitely.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a negative error code otherwise. -ETIMEDOUT will be specifically returned if the timeout kick-in instead of an actual event.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, uint64_t mgmt_event_mask, uint64_t *raised_event, const void **info, size_t *info_length, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: net_mgmt_event_wait_on_iface

briefdescription.para: Used to wait synchronously on an event mask for a specific iface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer on a valid network interface to listen event to
A mask of relevant events to wait on. Listened to events should be relevant to iface events and thus have the bit NET_MGMT_IFACE_BIT set.
a pointer on a uint32_t to get which event from the mask generated the event. Can be NULL if the caller is not interested in that information.
a valid pointer if user wants to get the information the event might bring along. NULL otherwise.
tells how long the info memory area is. Only valid if the info is not NULL.
A timeout delay. K_FOREVER can be used to wait indefinitely.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, a negative error code otherwise. -ETIMEDOUT will be specifically returned if the timeout kick-in instead of an actual event.

briefdescription.para: Used by the core of the network stack to initialize the network event processing.
Event emitted when the network interface goes down.
Event emitted when the network interface goes up.
Event emitted when the network interface is taken down manually.
Event emitted when the network interface goes up manually.
Event emitted when an IPv6 address is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 address is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 multicast address is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 multicast address is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 prefix is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 prefix is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 multicast group is joined.
Event emitted when an IPv6 multicast group is left.
Event emitted when an IPv6 router is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 router is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 route is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 route is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 duplicate address detection succeeds.
Event emitted when an IPv6 duplicate address detection fails.
Event emitted when an IPv6 neighbor is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 neighbor is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv6 DHCP client starts.
Event emitted when an IPv6 DHCP client address is bound.
Event emitted when an IPv6 DHCP client is stopped.
IPv6 address is deprecated.
IPv6 Privacy extension is enabled.
IPv6 Privacy extension is disabled.
IPv6 Privacy extension filter is added.
IPv6 Privacy extension filter is removed.
IPv6 Path MTU is changed.
Event emitted when an IPv4 address is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 address is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 multicast address is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 multicast address is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 router is added to the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 router is removed from the system.
Event emitted when an IPv4 DHCP client is started.
Event emitted when an IPv4 DHCP client address is bound.
Event emitted when an IPv4 DHCP client is stopped.
Event emitted when an IPv4 multicast group is joined.
Event emitted when an IPv4 multicast group is left.
Event emitted when an IPv4 address conflict detection succeeds.
Event emitted when an IPv4 address conflict detection fails.
Event emitted when an IPv4 address conflict was detected after the address was confirmed as safe to use.

detaileddescription.para: It's up to the application to determine on how to act in such case.

briefdescription.para: IPv4 Path MTU is changed.
Event emitted when the system is considered to be connected.

detaileddescription.para: The connected in this context means that the network interface is up, and the interface has either IPv4 or IPv6 address assigned to it.

briefdescription.para: Event emitted when the system is no longer connected.

detaileddescription.para: Typically this means that network connectivity is lost either by the network interface is going down, or the interface has no longer an IP address etc.

briefdescription.para: Event raised when IPv4 network connectivity is available.
Event emitted when IPv4 network connectivity is lost.
Event emitted when IPv6 network connectivity is available.
Event emitted when IPv6 network connectivity is lost.
Event emitted when a DNS server is added to the system.
Event emitted when a DNS server is removed from the system.
Event emitted when the system hostname is changed.
Network packet capture is started.
Network packet capture is stopped.
Event emitted when VPN network connectivity is available.
Event emitted when VPN network connectivity is lost.
Event emitted when a VPN peer is added to the system.
Event emitted when a VPN peer is removed from the system.
Generate a network management event.

parameterdescription.para: Management event identifier
Network interface
Any additional data for the event
Length of the additional data.

memberdef.name: NET_MGMT_DEFINE_REQUEST_HANDLER
memberdef.initializer: extern int net_mgmt_##_mgmt_request(uint64_t mgmt_request, \ struct net_if *iface, \ void *data, size_t len)

briefdescription.para: Declare a request handler function for the given network event.

parameterdescription.para: Management event identifier

briefdescription.para: Create a request handler function for the given network event.

parameterdescription.para: Management event identifier
Function for handling this event

briefdescription.para: Define a static network event handler.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the event handler.

A mask of network events on which the passed handler should be called in case those events come. Note that only the command part is treated as a mask, matching one to several commands. Layer and layer code will be made of an exact match. This means that in order to receive events from multiple layers, one must have multiple listeners registered, one for each layer being listened.
The function to be called upon network events being emitted.
User data passed to the handler being called on network events.

briefdescription.para: Network Management.
